recent_calls.summary: William called to confirm that the wiring for his new central heating and air unit passed inspection and that no extra work was done. He provided his updated service address and preferred a phone call follow-up to clear up the concern. The agent confirmed the details, scheduled a callback, and received positive feedback from William about the professionalism of the service.

The user requested paperwork related to their American Home Shield home warranty for their air conditioning system. The agent confirmed the request and sought more details about the specific issue, but the call ended before completion.

The user, Donda, called to report a leaking HVAC system installed the previous day and requested a repair. She also wanted to schedule rewiring of the furnace for Friday morning, as it was the only day she could take off work. The agent documented the issues and arranged for a callback from a representative the next business day to assist with scheduling.

The user called to inquire about maintenance services for their heating and air conditioning system and asked about pricing. The agent explained that pricing varies and offered to have a representative follow up, but the user declined and said they would call back.

The user, Whitney, called to reschedule a plumbing appointment for a water heater flush as part of their maintenance plan. The agent successfully booked the appointment for May 5th at 10 AM and clarified the dispatch fee situation. The call ended with the user declining to provide feedback.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.
The call was very brief and ended abruptly after the agent asked for the user's service address ZIP code. The user did not provide any information before hanging up.
The agent greeted the user and asked for the service address ZIP code, but the user hung up immediately without responding.
The call was very brief, with the agent attempting to start the conversation by asking for the user's service address ZIP code, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user called to discuss a recent invoice and was promptly transferred to the billing team for assistance. The agent explained they could not access billing information but ensured the user was connected to the appropriate department.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user returned a phone call and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent acknowledged the callback and facilitated the transfer without further interaction.
The agent greeted the user and asked for the service address ZIP code, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user called and was quickly transferred to a live customer service representative for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer promptly.
The user called and requested to speak with Cece. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to the appropriate person for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about replacing a gas pool heater for an in-ground pool and whether installation services are offered. The agent explained that they mainly handle HVAC and standard water heaters but offered to take the user's information for further review. The call ended shortly after without completing the information exchange.

The user, Kari, called to schedule routine maintenance through American Home Shield. The agent confirmed the details and informed Kari that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact her shortly. Kari declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

The user called to inquire if O'Fallon, Missouri is within the service area for plumbing services. After confirming the location and service type, the agent transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user, Michael, called to return calls and set up an appointment. The agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for appointment scheduling.

The user, Reggie, called to inquire about becoming an executive member with the Better Business Bureau and requested to be connected with the appropriate contact person. The agent, Sophie, took down the user's contact information and assured that the request would be forwarded to the right team member. The call ended positively with the user expressing satisfaction and no further questions.

Jim Lane called to request a salt delivery and a technical service appointment for his water purification system due to strange noises and a flashing sensor box. The agent scheduled a service appointment for the next day at 10 AM and confirmed the details with Jim. Jim was satisfied with the quick and helpful service provided by the agent.
The agent attempted to engage the user by asking for their service address ZIP code, but the user did not respond and hung up shortly after.

The user called to speak with an associate about an electrical wiring issue involving the wrong gauge wire installed on a unit. The agent confirmed it was not an emergency and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Roberta called to schedule a home warranty service appointment for her air conditioning system that is not cooling. The agent confirmed the details and scheduled the appointment for the next day, noting that Roberta did not have the dispatch number. Roberta declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

The user called to schedule a service appointment for their air conditioner, which is not producing enough cool air. The agent provided available time slots, and the user selected an appointment for the next day at 8 AM. The booking was successfully made, and the user was informed about a standard dispatch fee, though they declined to provide feedback.

The user called about receiving repeated texts to fill out a form despite having already done so and mentioned an appointment scheduled through American Home Shield for a heating and cooling checkup. The agent attempted to assist but transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The call ended with a successful transfer.

The user called about a cooling unit estimate and expressed a desire to speak with someone directly. The agent attempted to gather more details but ultimately transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to confirm that the office received the contract she signed. The agent assured her that the request would be passed to the team, who would follow up with her. The call ended with the user acknowledging the information and no further requests.

The user called to speak with Cole regarding a repair to their air conditioner involving the replacement of the evaporator coil, which was quoted at $3600. The agent attempted to connect the user to Cole for further assistance and successfully transferred the call. The user expressed a clear intent to discuss the repair costs with Cole.

The user, Champeka Linskog, called to inquire about an AC tune-up service request for 1360 Whitefield Lane. The agent could not find an appointment on file and offered to transfer the user to a live representative, which the user accepted. The call was successfully transferred to another agent for further assistance.

The user called to have Budget resend the most recent air conditioning estimate to their email. The agent confirmed the user's details, including service address, contact information, and service type, and assured that someone from Budget would reach out shortly. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.

The user, Fatmata Alonatsi, called to inquire about the technician's arrival time in Nashville. The agent confirmed the appointment with technician Andrew scheduled between 12:30 and 1:30 PM. The call ended with the user being transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to confirm the time of their appointment scheduled for April 30th. The agent confirmed the appointment time and agreed to notify the technician to call the user upon arrival since the user would be home after 2:15 PM. The call ended with the user's request noted and no further questions.

The user called to request a callback because a technician appeared to be leaving. The user indicated the situation was an emergency but did not specify any active damage. The agent attempted to gather more details about the emergency.

The user, Darius, called to get an update on a previous service request but was transferred to a live representative for further assistance. The call was brief and ended with a successful transfer to another agent.

The user called to reschedule a cooling service appointment due to the system putting out warm air. The agent collected all necessary details and confirmed the reschedule for Monday, May 4th. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user, Fatmata, called to confirm the time of a follow-up appointment for both heating and air conditioning services. The agent noted the preferred arrival time of 9 or 10 AM and confirmed the service address and details. The call ended with the user declining to provide feedback and the agent wishing her a good day.

William called to report an issue with the wire gauge from his outside condenser to the circuit box, which a professional recommended upgrading. He requested a follow-up service early next week, and the agent confirmed the service address and details. William provided positive feedback about the call experience and agreed to be contacted for scheduling.

The user called to check the status of their cooling system order and requested a follow-up phone call as soon as possible on the next business day. The agent confirmed the service address and phone number, noted the user's preference to not receive information about maintenance programs, and assured that the team will follow up accordingly.

David called to report that someone was supposed to be at his unit at 9 AM but no one showed up, and he has been unable to get a response despite multiple attempts. The agent apologized and tried to clarify if the issue was an emergency or related to rescheduling. The call ended abruptly before resolution.

The user called to inquire if the technician was at their house for an AC repair service. The agent explained that real-time technician location updates are not available after-hours and asked for more details about the AC issue. The call ended shortly after without resolution.

The user called after hours requesting to speak to a representative. The agent informed the user that a representative was not available but offered to take their information for a callback on the next business day. The user did not provide their name before hanging up.

Loretta called to reschedule her cooling system service appointment due to a conflict with a doctor's appointment. The agent noted her preference for the appointment to be after 10 or 11 AM and assured her the team would follow up to confirm. Loretta provided positive feedback but expressed some anxiety about not receiving immediate confirmation of the schedule change.

The user called Budget's after-hours assistant to request an updated quote for a furnace service, specifically needing the quote to reflect updated dates for pricing. The agent engaged with the user to clarify the details of the update needed.

The user called to inquire if a technician had come to their house for a cooling system service appointment scheduled for that day. The agent attempted to assist but ultimately transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The user wanted to confirm the technician's visit status.

Paula from American Home Shield called Budget to follow up on the status of a work order for Angela Severe's central electric AC at 2401 Quinn Drive, St. Louis, MO. The homeowner has accepted the out-of-pocket cost, and Paula requested a status update on the parts, providing the dispatch number and contact information for follow-up. The agent confirmed the details and assured Paula that the request would be passed to the team for a response within the day.

Ryan called to report an issue with his cooling system not blowing cold air and wanted to schedule a warranty service appointment. The agent collected all necessary details and confirmed the information with Ryan, who declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests. The call ended with the agent assuring that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact Ryan soon.
